title: Kelly Greenhill on Office Hours
summary: Kelly Greenhill, an Associate Professor at Tufts University and a Research Fellow with the Belfer Center’s International Security Project, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about refugees, building walls, R2P, and weapons of mass migration.
body: Kelly Greenhill, an Associate Professor at Tufts University and a Research Fellow with the Belfer Center’s International Security Project, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about refugees, building walls, R2P, and weapons of mass migration. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 4 Kelly Greenhill: Border Walls: How Effective? 2 of 4 Kelly Greenhill: Weapons of Mass Migration 3 of 4 Kelly Greenhill: Refugees and Radicalization 4 of 4 Kelly Greenhill: What is R2P? Kelly M. Greenhill is a research fellow in the Belfer Center's International Security Program and an Associate Professor (with tenure) at Tufts University. Her research focuses on foreign policy, the use of military force, and what are frequently called "new security challenges," including civil wars; the use of forced migration as a weapon; military intervention and (counter-) insurgency; and international crime as a challenge to domestic governance. She holds a Ph.D. and an S.M. from M.I.T., a C.S.S. from Harvard University, and a B.A. (with distinction and highest honors) in Political Economy and in Scandinavian Studies from the University of California at Berkeley. Greenhill also serves as Chair of the Conflict, Security, and Public Policy Working Group at the International Security Program and as Associate Editor of the journal International Security . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In this interview, Kelly Greenhill discusses the concept of 'weapons of mass migration,' exploring how forced migration can be used as a coercive tool in international relations. She analyzes the effectiveness of border walls, the complex relationship between refugees and radicalization, and the principles of the Responsibility to Protect (R2P) doctrine. Her research focuses on new security challenges, including civil wars, military intervention, and the use of migration as a strategic instrument, situating these issues within broader foreign policy and international security debates.
result.main_content_zh: 凯莉·格林希尔在访谈中探讨了“大规模移民武器化”的概念，分析了强迫移民如何被用作国际关系中的胁迫工具。她评估了边境墙的有效性，讨论了难民与激进主义之间的复杂关联，并阐释了“保护的责任”（R2P）原则。她的研究聚焦于新型安全挑战，包括内战、军事干预以及将移民作为战略手段等问题，并将这些议题置于更广泛的外交政策和国际安全辩论的框架之中。
